findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading order. The letterhead information (PAT McCRORY Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART Secretary, TRACY DAVIS Director, Energy Mineral & Land Resources) and the state footer (address, phone) appear inline as body text between content sections, duplicating the PDF's page-decoration elements within the reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the letterhead header once at the top as page decoration. In the HTML (page 1), the state footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality...') appears mid-page, followed by a colored bar, then the header info (PAT McCRORY Governor, etc.) is repeated as inline text content before the MEMORANDUM heading begins. A sighted reader encounters this duplicated header/footer content as if it were document body text.

findings.description: Footer text from PDF page boundaries rendered inline within the HTML document body, disrupting the continuous reading flow between Section I/II content.
findings.evidence: At the boundary corresponding to PDF pages 2-3 in the HTML (visible at the bottom of HTML page 3), the state footer ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mineral and Land Resources | 512 N. Salisbury Street...') and a colored decorative bar appear inline between the Section II oral comments content, interrupting the reading flow.

findings.description: A full-page-width image (612pt) is inserted mid-content between the introductory MEMORANDUM section and the History/Background section. This likely represents a decorative page footer bar or full-page scan from PDF page 1, placed within the body flow rather than being omitted or placed in a footer.
findings.evidence:
HTML: appears between the closing of the TOC and the
for 'I. History/Background'. No corresponding standalone substantive image exists at this position in the PDF.
